subject: Surgical wakeup-script edit request — retire `wakeup gauge` per ADR-0001
context: E2E build Sprint 0 (2026-05-06) executed ADR-0001 (Gauge identity reconciliation: Task-tool sub-agent, NOT hub persona). All ratification + migration cleanup is in flight. The wakeup script at ~/MyDocs/scripts/wakeup is the only remaining surface in your domain, hence this Collab MSG.
ask: Apply the surgical 2-line edit below to retire the `wakeup gauge` flow. Non-blocking for Sprint 0 close — the hub-presence retirement is achieved by deleting ~/.claude/personas/gauge.md (Gauge's lane), regardless of whether the wakeup launcher still recognizes the name. This is consistency cleanup, not a critical-path item.

lines_to_remove.reason: PERSONAS array entry — drives `wakeup gauge` launch + hub channel binding on port 8811. Per ADR-0001, Gauge has no hub presence; this entry is now misleading.

lines_to_remove.current: echo "  wakeup gauge --here          # Gauge testing in current project"
lines_to_remove.reason: Help-text example — should not advertise a flow that is no longer supported.

proposed_edit.diff_summary: Remove 2 lines. No structural changes. Other personas (Sarah, Cord, Fulton, Dash, Herald, Number2, AIDI, etc.) unaffected.

rationale: ADR-0001 (3-corner consensus Quinn/Gauge/Archie 2026-05-06; Toby explicitly designated TEAM-DECIDE per Number2 directive 2026-05-06T14:51) restored D-001 (2026-04-14 Toby+Archie). Gauge runs as a Claude Code Task-tool sub-agent with definition at ~/.claude/agents/gauge.md. Quinn invokes via Task tool with subagent_type: "gauge", one spawn per UC. Cross-team handoffs flow Gauge → contractual JSON return → Quinn-as-relay → target persona via hub. Hub presence is unforced cost for episodic E2E test work. The wakeup-launcher artifact is the last drift-evidence in your domain.

references.sub_agent_def: ~/.claude/agents/gauge.md (canonical post-migration; line 14 verbatim: "You are NOT a hub persona.")

alternatives_considered: Quinn applies the edit directly: rejected — ~/MyDocs/scripts/ is your domain per code-boundary discipline.
Gauge applies the edit: rejected — same code-boundary reason; Gauge is sub-agent + framework-scoped only.
Defer to Wednesday standards pass: viable but the drift-evidence preservation argument (Herald's corpus) doesn't extend to active launchers; this is a runtime artifact, not a doc-evidence specimen.
if_no_response: Carry-over into Sprint 0 close-out doc as 'pending Gotan'. No re-pings; you'll surface naturally on next session-cycle.
thank_you: Thanks for keeping the cross-half routing clean. The fact that this is a 2-line ask (and not a debate) is itself a sign the Sprint 0 sequencing worked.
